articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Key Provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act Amendments
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today delivered a landmark judgment, largely upholding the constitutional validity of recent amendments to the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The ruling addressed petitions challenging aspects related to data processing by state entities and the broad powers granted to the Data Protection Board.
• The court emphasized the balance between national security interests, legitimate state functions, and individual privacy rights, providing clarifications on certain clauses.
• Legal experts note the judgment's significant implications for privacy jurisprudence and the future of digital governance in India.
• This decision is expected to streamline the implementation of the DPDP Act, fostering a more robust framework for data protection.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies.
keyTerms: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act)
prelimsPoints: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) 2023 aims to regulate processing of digital personal data.
The Act establishes the Data Protection Board of India.
Key principles include purpose limitation, data minimization, and accountability.
Article 21 of the Indian Constitution guarantees the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right (Puttaswamy judgment).
mainsPoints: Discuss the balancing act between individual privacy, national security, and state functions in the digital age.
Analyze the role of the judiciary in upholding fundamental rights while allowing for legislative action on emerging technologies.
Evaluate the effectiveness and challenges of implementing data protection laws in a diverse digital economy like India's.
Examine the implications of the DPDP Act for businesses, government agencies, and individual citizens.
articles.imageDescription: Gavel on legal documents with a blurred image of digital data streams in the background.
articles.trivia: India's first attempt at a dedicated data protection law dates back to the Justice B.N. Srikrishna Committee report in 2018.

articles.headline: RBI Monetary Policy Committee Maintains Repo Rate Amidst Persistent Inflation Concerns
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) today announced its decision to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• The decision was taken unanimously, citing persistent inflationary pressures, particularly from food prices, despite a moderation in core inflation.
• RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das reiterated the central bank's commitment to withdrawing accommodation gradually to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Analysts expect this stance to continue for the near term, with potential rate cuts only if inflation shows sustained decline and global economic conditions stabilize.
• The MPC also maintained its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance, signaling caution in liquidity management.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary policy.
prelimsPoints: Repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member body responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate in India.
The primary objective of monetary policy in India is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Inflation target for RBI is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth in the current global and domestic scenario.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools like the repo rate in managing demand-side inflation in India.
Evaluate the impact of persistent food inflation on household budgets and overall economic stability.
Examine the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance and its implications for liquidity in the financial system.
articles.imageDescription: RBI logo with graphs showing economic indicators in the background.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee was constituted in 2016 to bring more transparency and accountability to monetary policy decisions.

articles.headline: India and Japan Deepen Strategic Partnership with New Defence and Technology Cooperation Deals
articles.explainer:
• India and Japan today concluded a series of agreements aimed at enhancing bilateral defence cooperation, including joint development of military hardware and technology transfer.
• The deals were signed during the visit of Japan's Defence Minister to New Delhi, emphasizing shared strategic interests in the Indo-Pacific region.
• Key areas of collaboration include maritime security, cybersecurity, and emerging defence technologies.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to a free and open Indo-Pacific and discussed coordinated efforts in multilateral forums like the Quad.
• This partnership is seen as crucial for regional stability and counterbalancing growing geopolitical complexities.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and Japan are members of the Quad (Quadrilateral Security Dialogue) along with the US and Australia.
Both countries participate in joint military exercises such as 'Dharma Guardian' (army) and 'JIMEX' (navy).
Japan is a key partner in India's Act East Policy.
Indo-Pacific region is crucial for global trade and security.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India-Japan strategic partnership for regional stability in the Indo-Pacific.
Discuss how defence and technology cooperation strengthens India's 'Act East Policy' and strategic autonomy.
Evaluate the role of bilateral partnerships in multilateral frameworks like the Quad in addressing common security challenges.
Examine the potential benefits and challenges of joint defence production and technology transfer between the two nations.
articles.imageDescription: Indian and Japanese flags superimposed over a map of the Indo-Pacific region.
articles.trivia: Japan is a major investor in India's infrastructure projects, including the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail corridor.

articles.headline: Government Unveils Ambitious New Targets for National Green Hydrogen Mission by 2030
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet today approved revised and more ambitious targets for India's National Green Hydrogen Mission, aiming for significant production capacity by 2030.
• The updated plan seeks to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, reducing reliance on fossil fuels.
• New incentives for research and development, manufacturing of electrolysers, and development of green hydrogen corridors were announced.
• The mission is expected to create numerous jobs, attract substantial investment, and contribute significantly to India's climate goals.
• Emphasizing a phased approach, the government aims to reduce the cost of green hydrogen and make it competitive with conventional fuels.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
India launched the National Green Hydrogen Mission in 2023.
The mission aims to reduce fossil fuel imports and achieve net-zero emissions by 2070.
Electrolysers are key components in green hydrogen production.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate commitments.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
Evaluate the potential impact of the National Green Hydrogen Mission on India's economy, job creation, and industrial landscape.
Examine the role of government policies and incentives in fostering a competitive green hydrogen ecosystem.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen plant with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, but green hydrogen is specifically produced using renewable energy sources.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite for Enhanced Mapping
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today successfully launched a new advanced Earth Observation Satellite (EOS) onboard the PSLV-C59 rocket from Sriharikota.
• The satellite, designated EOS-08, is equipped with high-resolution optical sensors for detailed mapping and monitoring of Earth's surface.
• Its applications include urban planning, disaster management, agricultural assessment, and environmental monitoring.
• This mission further strengthens India's remote sensing capabilities and its efforts in providing crucial geospatial data for various national developmental programs.
• The launch marks another milestone for ISRO in expanding its fleet of operational Earth observation satellites.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: PSLV is ISRO's third-generation launch vehicle, primarily used for launching Earth Observation and remote sensing satellites.
Earth Observation Satellites provide data for various applications like weather forecasting, agriculture, disaster management.
ISRO is headquartered in Bengaluru.
Remote sensing involves acquiring information about an object or phenomenon without making physical contact.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multifarious applications of Earth Observation Satellites in India's developmental planning and disaster management.
Analyze how ISRO's indigenous space capabilities contribute to India's self-reliance in critical technologies and national security.
Evaluate the role of geospatial data and remote sensing in achieving sustainable development goals in India.
Examine the challenges and future prospects for India's space program in the context of increasing private sector participation and global competition.
articles.imageDescription: PSLV rocket launching into space with an Earth Observation Satellite.
articles.trivia: The PSLV holds a world record for launching the highest number of satellites (104) in a single mission in 2017.

articles.headline: Uttar Pradesh MLA Sudhakar Singh Passes Away at 67
articles.explainer:
• Sudhakar Singh, a sitting Member of Legislative Assembly (MLA) from Uttar Pradesh, passed away today at the age of 67.
• He was a prominent political figure in the region, having been elected to the state assembly in 2023.
• Singh was known for his dedication to local issues and his advocacy for rural development.
• His demise leaves a vacancy in the Uttar Pradesh Legislative Assembly, necessitating a by-election.
• Political leaders across party lines expressed condolences on his passing, acknowledging his contributions to public service.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: An MLA is a representative elected by the voters of an electoral district (constituency) to the legislature of a state government.
A by-election is held to fill a vacancy arising during the term of a current Member of Parliament or a Member of the Legislative Assembly.
The minimum age to be an MLA is 25 years.
Uttar Pradesh has a bicameral legislature, consisting of the Legislative Assembly (Vidhan Sabha) and the Legislative Council (Vidhan Parishad).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of state legislative assemblies in India's federal structure and democratic governance.
Analyze the process and implications of by-elections for political dynamics at the state level.
Examine the role and responsibilities of an MLA in representing their constituency and contributing to state policy-making.
Evaluate the challenges faced by state legislatures in effective law-making and oversight.
articles.imageDescription: A blurred image of the Uttar Pradesh Legislative Assembly building.
articles.trivia: The Uttar Pradesh Legislative Assembly is the largest state legislative assembly in India, with 403 elected members.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'PM-POSHAN 2.0' for Enhanced Nutritional Support to School Children
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government today launched 'PM-POSHAN 2.0', an upgraded and expanded version of the Mid-Day Meal Scheme, focusing on holistic nutritional support for school children.
• The scheme aims to cover all students from pre-primary to class VIII in government and government-aided schools across the country.
• Key features include an emphasis on locally sourced, nutritious food, supplementary nutrition in aspirational districts, and promoting 'Tithi Bhojan' for community participation.
• The initiative seeks to combat malnutrition, improve school enrollment and attendance, and foster better learning outcomes.
• A strong monitoring framework leveraging technology will be implemented to ensure efficient delivery and accountability.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: PM-POSHAN (Pradhan Mantri Poshan Shakti Nirman) is the new name for the Mid-Day Meal Scheme.
The scheme aims to provide hot cooked meals to children in government and government-aided schools.
It is a Centrally Sponsored Scheme.
Tithi Bhojan is a concept where people celebrate special occasions by contributing food to children in schools.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution and significance of the Mid-Day Meal Scheme in addressing child malnutrition and promoting education in India.
Analyze the potential impact of PM-POSHAN 2.0 on improving health and educational outcomes for vulnerable children.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing large-scale nutritional programs, including logistics, quality control, and community participation.
Examine the role of technology and local sourcing in enhancing the efficiency and effectiveness of such welfare schemes.
articles.imageDescription: Children eating a meal in a school setting, depicting a welfare scheme.
articles.trivia: The Mid-Day Meal Scheme was first launched in 1995 as a centrally sponsored scheme.

articles.headline: India's Merchandise Exports Show Resilience Despite Global Headwinds, Data Indicates
articles.explainer:
• Latest trade data released today indicates that India's merchandise exports have demonstrated notable resilience, registering a modest growth despite a challenging global economic environment.
• Non-petroleum and non-gem & jewellery sectors were key contributors to this positive trend, signaling diversification of India's export basket.
• Government initiatives like Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes and efforts to reduce logistics costs are attributed to supporting exporters.
• However, concerns remain regarding demand slowdown in major economies and ongoing geopolitical tensions.
• The Ministry of Commerce expects continued growth, focusing on new markets and product categories.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
Investment models.
prelimsPoints: Merchandise exports refer to the export of physical goods.
Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes aim to boost domestic manufacturing and exports in specific sectors.
Balance of Trade is the difference between a country's exports and imports of goods.
Global headwinds include factors like inflation, recession fears, and geopolitical conflicts affecting global trade.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to the resilience of India's merchandise exports amidst global economic uncertainties.
Discuss the role of government policies, such as PLI schemes and trade agreements, in boosting India's export competitiveness.
Evaluate the challenges faced by Indian exporters in diversifying markets and products, and suggest policy interventions.
Examine the impact of global trade protectionism and geopolitical shifts on India's export strategy and economic growth.
articles.imageDescription: Shipping containers stacked at a port, symbolizing global trade.
articles.trivia: India aims to achieve merchandise exports of $1 trillion by 2030.

articles.headline: India Advocates for Global South Concerns at G20 Sherpa Meeting in Brazil
articles.explainer:
• India's G20 Sherpa today strongly advocated for the concerns and priorities of the Global South during the ongoing G20 Sherpa meeting in Brazil.
• Key issues raised included equitable access to finance, climate justice, sustainable development, and reforms of multilateral institutions.
• India emphasized the need for a human-centric approach to development and highlighted its initiatives in digital public infrastructure.
• The meeting is part of Brazil's G20 presidency preparations, focusing on building consensus for the upcoming Leaders' Summit.
• India's active participation aims to ensure that the voices of developing nations are adequately represented in global decision-making.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: G20 is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union.
The G20 Sherpa is the personal representative of the head of state or government of a G20 member country.
Brazil holds the G20 Presidency for 2025.
Global South refers to countries in Africa, Latin America, and developing Asia.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's growing role as a voice for the Global South in international forums like the G20.
Discuss the challenges faced by developing nations, such as climate financing and debt sustainability, and how G20 can address them.
Evaluate the significance of digital public infrastructure as a model for inclusive development, as advocated by India.
Examine the need for reforms in multilateral institutions to better reflect the geopolitical realities and aspirations of the Global South.
articles.imageDescription: G20 logo with flags of member countries, representing international cooperation.
articles.trivia: India hosted the G20 Summit in 2023, emphasizing the theme 'Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam' (One Earth, One Family, One Future).

articles.headline: Central Government Unveils New Policy for Urban Wetland Conservation and Management
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change today launched a comprehensive new policy aimed at the conservation and sustainable management of urban wetlands across India.
• The policy emphasizes integrated planning, community participation, and scientific restoration of these vital ecosystems within city limits.
• It mandates the identification, mapping, and notification of urban wetlands, along with guidelines for preventing encroachment and pollution.
• Urban wetlands play a crucial role in flood mitigation, groundwater recharge, biodiversity conservation, and climate regulation.
• The initiative seeks to strengthen the ecological resilience of urban areas and enhance their 'blue-green' infrastructure.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
prelimsPoints: Wetlands are areas where water covers the soil, or is present either at or near the surface of the soil all year or for varying periods of time during the year.
The Ramsar Convention on Wetlands is an international treaty for the conservation and sustainable use of wetlands.
Urban wetlands provide critical ecosystem services like flood control, water purification, and biodiversity support.
The Wetlands (Conservation and Management) Rules, 2017, provide a regulatory framework in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological and socio-economic significance of urban wetlands, particularly in the context of increasing urbanization in India.
Analyze the challenges in conserving urban wetlands, including encroachment, pollution, and lack of public awareness.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the new policy in ensuring sustainable management and restoration of these vital ecosystems.
Examine how urban wetland conservation can contribute to climate resilience and the development of 'smart cities' in India.
articles.imageDescription: A serene urban wetland with reeds and migratory birds, flanked by city buildings in the distance.
articles.trivia: World Wetlands Day is celebrated annually on February 2nd, marking the date of the adoption of the Convention on Wetlands in Ramsar, Iran, in 1971.

articles.headline: Border Roads Organisation Completes Construction of Strategic All-Weather Tunnel in Himalayas
articles.explainer:
• The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) today announced the completion of a crucial all-weather strategic tunnel in the rugged Himalayan terrain, significantly improving connectivity to border areas.
• The tunnel, located at a high altitude, will reduce travel time, enhance troop movement, and facilitate the transport of essential supplies throughout the year.
• This infrastructure project is part of India's broader strategy to strengthen border infrastructure and improve logistical capabilities along sensitive frontiers.
• It is expected to boost economic development in remote regions by enabling easier access for trade and tourism.
• The BRO utilized advanced engineering techniques to overcome challenging geological and climatic conditions during construction.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) constructs and maintains road networks in border areas and friendly neighboring countries.
BRO works under the Ministry of Defence.
Strategic tunnels like this are vital for ensuring year-round connectivity in snow-bound and high-altitude regions.
Improved border infrastructure is crucial for national security and economic development of border regions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of developing all-weather connectivity in India's border regions, particularly in the Himalayas.
Analyze the role of the Border Roads Organisation (BRO) in enhancing India's defence preparedness and logistical capabilities.
Evaluate the socio-economic impact of improved infrastructure on the lives of people residing in remote border areas.
Examine the challenges involved in constructing infrastructure in mountainous terrain and the technological advancements employed by BRO.
articles.imageDescription: A newly constructed tunnel entrance in a mountainous, snow-capped region.
articles.trivia: The Atal Tunnel, also constructed by BRO, is the world's longest highway tunnel above 10,000 feet.

articles.headline: Report Highlights Progress and Persistent Challenges in Achieving Gender Equality in Workforce
articles.explainer:
• A new report released by a leading think tank today lauded India's progress in certain aspects of women's participation in the workforce but also pointed out persistent challenges.
• The report noted improvements in women's enrollment in higher education and entry into certain professional fields.
• However, it highlighted significant disparities in leadership roles, wage gaps, and the impact of informal care responsibilities on women's careers.
• Policy recommendations included enhanced childcare support, flexible work arrangements, and stricter enforcement of equal pay legislation.
• The findings underscore the need for continued multi-faceted interventions to achieve true gender parity in the economic sphere.
gsSubTopics: Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Social empowerment, communalism, regionalism & secularism.
prelimsPoints: Female Labour Force Participation Rate (FLFPR) measures the proportion of working-age women who are employed or actively seeking employment.
Sustainable Development Goal 5 aims to achieve gender equality and empower all women and girls.
The Equal Remuneration Act, 1976, provides for payment of equal remuneration to men and women workers for same work or work of a similar nature.
Informal care responsibilities often disproportionately fall on women, impacting their career progression.
mainsPoints: Analyze the socio-economic factors contributing to the gender gap in India's workforce and its implications for national development.
Discuss the effectiveness of existing government policies and initiatives aimed at enhancing women's economic empowerment.
Evaluate the role of education, skill development, and changing societal attitudes in promoting gender equality in the workplace.
Suggest comprehensive strategies, including legislative, infrastructural, and cultural changes, to achieve gender parity in the Indian workforce.
articles.imageDescription: Diverse group of women professionals in a modern office setting.
articles.trivia: According to the World Economic Forum's Global Gender Gap Report, economic participation and opportunity is one of the key pillars assessed for gender parity.

articles.headline: NITI Aayog Releases Draft AI Ethics Guidelines for Public Consultation
articles.explainer:
• NITI Aayog today released a comprehensive set of draft guidelines on Artificial Intelligence (AI) ethics for public consultation, focusing on responsible AI development and deployment.
• The guidelines emphasize principles such as fairness, accountability, transparency, safety, and privacy in AI systems.
• They aim to foster innovation while mitigating potential risks associated with AI, including bias, discrimination, and misuse.
• The framework seeks to provide a common understanding and best practices for stakeholders across government, industry, and academia.
• This move is part of India's broader strategy to become a global leader in ethical and responsible AI.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: NITI Aayog is the premier policy 'Think Tank' of the Government of India.
AI ethics refers to the branch of ethics that studies the ethical issues raised by artificial intelligence.
Principles of responsible AI often include fairness, transparency, accountability, and privacy.
Algorithmic bias occurs when an algorithm produces results that are systematically prejudiced.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ethical challenges posed by the rapid advancement and deployment of Artificial Intelligence in various sectors.
Analyze the significance of NITI Aayog's draft AI ethics guidelines in shaping India's approach to responsible AI development.
Evaluate the balance between fostering innovation in AI and mitigating potential risks such as bias and privacy violations.
Examine how a robust AI ethics framework can contribute to public trust and ensure equitable benefits from AI technologies.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract representation of AI neural networks with a human hand reaching out, symbolizing ethical interaction.
articles.trivia: NITI Aayog has been instrumental in formulating India's national strategy for Artificial Intelligence, emphasizing 'AI for All'.

articles.headline: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Textiles Attracts Significant Investment Proposals
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Textiles today announced that the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Textiles has attracted a significant number of investment proposals, indicating strong industry interest.
• The scheme, aimed at boosting domestic manufacturing and exports of Man-Made Fibre (MMF) apparel, MMF fabrics, and technical textiles, is showing promising early results.
• The investments are expected to create new jobs, enhance India's competitiveness in the global textile market, and move towards a higher value chain.
• Government support through the PLI scheme is crucial for modernizing the textile sector and attracting both domestic and foreign investment.
• This initiative aligns with the 'Make in India' vision and aims to position India as a global textile manufacturing hub.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
Investment models.
prelimsPoints: The PLI scheme offers incentives on incremental sales from products manufactured in India.
It aims to make Indian manufacturing globally competitive.
Man-Made Fibres (MMF) include polyester, viscose, nylon, etc.
Technical textiles are functional fabrics used in various industries like automotive, medical, and agriculture.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme and its potential to transform India's manufacturing sector, specifically textiles.
Analyze the challenges faced by the Indian textile industry and how the PLI scheme addresses them to boost competitiveness and exports.
Evaluate the socio-economic impact of increased investment and job creation in the textile sector, particularly for women's employment.
Examine the role of government support in moving India up the global value chain in key manufacturing sectors.
articles.imageDescription: Textile machinery in a modern factory setting.
articles.trivia: India is one of the largest producers of textiles and apparel in the world, with the industry being a significant employer.

articles.headline: Winter Session of Parliament Expected to Prioritize Key Legislative Reforms
articles.explainer:
• Preparations are underway for the upcoming Winter Session of Parliament, expected to commence in early December, with the government signaling a packed legislative agenda.
• Key bills anticipated for discussion and passage include reforms related to labor laws, digital infrastructure, and environmental protection.
• The opposition is gearing up to raise issues concerning economic slowdown, inflation, and federalism, promising a lively session.
• Both houses of Parliament are expected to deliberate on crucial policy matters and conduct oversight of government functioning.
• The session will be critical for advancing the government's reform agenda and addressing pressing national concerns.
gsSubTopics: Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: The Indian Parliament typically has three sessions in a year: Budget Session, Monsoon Session, and Winter Session.
The maximum gap between two sessions of Parliament cannot be more than six months.
A bill becomes an Act after being passed by both houses of Parliament and receiving presidential assent.
Question Hour and Zero Hour are important parliamentary devices for holding the government accountable.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of parliamentary sessions in India's democratic framework, particularly the Winter Session.
Analyze the role of legislative reforms in driving economic growth and social development in India.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the Parliament in ensuring effective deliberation and passage of bills amidst political differences.
Examine the mechanisms of parliamentary oversight and their importance in holding the executive accountable.
articles.imageDescription: The Parliament House of India building at dusk.
articles.trivia: The first Lok Sabha was constituted on 17 April 1952.

articles.headline: India-EU Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Show Positive Momentum Amidst Key Differences
articles.explainer:
• Negotiations for the ambitious India-European Union Free Trade Agreement (FTA) are reportedly gaining positive momentum, with both sides expressing optimism for a breakthrough.
• Senior officials from India and the EU met today to discuss key chapters, including market access for goods, services, and investment protection.
• While significant progress has been made, differences persist on issues like tariffs on certain agricultural products, intellectual property rights, and sustainability clauses.
• A successful FTA could unlock substantial economic opportunities, boosting trade and investment between two of the world's largest economies.
• Both parties reiterated their commitment to concluding a balanced and comprehensive agreement.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: A Free Trade Agreement (FTA) is a pact between two or more countries to reduce barriers to imports and exports among them.
The European Union (EU) is a political and economic union of 27 member states.
India is the EU's 10th largest trading partner.
Tariffs are taxes imposed on imported goods and services.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of a comprehensive Free Trade Agreement between India and the European Union.
Discuss the major points of contention and convergence in the ongoing India-EU FTA negotiations.
Evaluate the potential benefits and challenges for key Indian sectors if the FTA is successfully implemented.
Examine how India balances its economic interests with concerns regarding intellectual property rights and sustainability clauses in trade agreements.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and the European Union intertwined, symbolizing cooperation.
articles.trivia: The EU is India's third-largest trading partner, accounting for 10.8% of India's total trade in goods in 2021-22.

articles.headline: India's Q2 GDP Growth Figures Indicate Robust Economic Momentum, Analysts Say
articles.explainer:
• Preliminary estimates for India's Q2 (July-September) GDP growth, released today, suggest robust economic momentum, exceeding many analyst expectations.
• Key drivers included strong domestic demand, a revival in manufacturing, and healthy performance in the services sector.
• The figures provide a positive outlook amidst global economic uncertainties, reinforcing India's position as a fast-growing major economy.
• Government policy support, capital expenditure, and private consumption are cited as crucial factors contributing to this growth.
• While inflation remains a concern, the growth numbers offer confidence in India's economic resilience.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
prelimsPoints: Gross Domestic Product (GDP) is the total monetary or market value of all the finished goods and services produced within a country's borders in a specific time period.
India's financial year runs from April 1 to March 31. Q2 refers to the July-September quarter.
Domestic demand is a key component of GDP, reflecting consumption and investment within the country.
The National Statistical Office (NSO) under the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation releases GDP data.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key factors driving India's economic growth in the current fiscal year, particularly the role of domestic demand and government expenditure.
Discuss the challenges posed by global economic slowdown and inflation in sustaining India's growth momentum.
Evaluate the significance of robust GDP growth for employment generation, poverty reduction, and overall development goals in India.
Examine the policy measures that can further boost manufacturing and services sectors to ensure inclusive and sustainable economic expansion.
articles.imageDescription: Upward trending graph representing economic growth with Indian currency notes.
articles.trivia: India is projected to become the world's third-largest economy by 2027.

articles.headline: Review Meeting on National Education Policy (NEP) Implementation Highlights Progress and Gaps
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education today held a high-level review meeting to assess the progress of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation across states and institutions.
• The review highlighted significant strides in curriculum reforms, teacher training, and integrating technology into education.
• However, challenges remain in infrastructure development, digital divide in remote areas, and achieving universal foundational literacy and numeracy.
• The meeting emphasized the need for accelerated efforts, increased budgetary allocation, and greater collaboration between central and state governments.
• NEP 2020 aims to transform India's education system to meet the demands of the 21st century.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: The National Education Policy 2020 replaced the 1986 National Policy on Education.
NEP 2020 aims for universalization of education from pre-school to secondary level.
It proposes a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure.
Foundational Literacy and Numeracy (FLN) is a key focus area under NEP 2020.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of the National Education Policy 2020 in revamping India's education system.
Analyze the progress and challenges encountered in the implementation of NEP 2020 across different levels of education.
Evaluate the role of technology and teacher training in achieving the goals of foundational literacy and holistic learning.
Examine the importance of federal cooperation and adequate funding in ensuring the successful and equitable implementation of national education policies.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom engaged in learning, symbolizing education reform.
articles.trivia: NEP 2020 aims to increase the Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) in higher education to 50% by 2035.

highlights: Supreme Court upholds key provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act amendments, setting a precedent for digital governance.
RBI's Monetary Policy Committee keeps the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% due to persistent inflation concerns, maintaining a cautious stance.
India and Japan strengthen strategic partnership with new defence and technology cooperation agreements, focusing on Indo-Pacific security.
Government unveils ambitious new targets for the National Green Hydrogen Mission by 2030, aiming for global leadership in clean energy.
ISRO successfully launches an advanced Earth Observation Satellite (EOS-08) for enhanced mapping and remote sensing applications.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023, consider the following statements:
1. It applies to the processing of digital personal data within India.
2. It establishes the Data Protection Board of India as an appellate body against decisions of data fiduciaries.
3. The Act defines 'personal data' as any data that relates to an identified or identifiable individual.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The DPDP Act, 2023, applies to the processing of digital personal data within India. Statement 2 is incorrect. The Data Protection Board of India is primarily a body to adjudicate compliance with the Act, not an appellate body against decisions of data fiduciaries (though appeals against its own orders can go to TDSAT). Statement 3 is correct. The Act defines 'personal data' as any data about an individual who is identifiable by or in relation to such data.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member body, headed by the RBI Governor, which is entrusted with the task of fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) to achieve the inflation target.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about Green Hydrogen is/are correct?
1. It is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy sources.
2. Its production process emits significant greenhouse gases.
3. India's National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to reduce the country's reliance on fossil fuel imports.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using electricity from renewable sources like solar or wind power. Statement 2 is incorrect. The key characteristic of green hydrogen production is that it has near-zero greenhouse gas emissions. Statement 3 is correct. A primary objective of India's National Green Hydrogen Mission is to reduce dependence on fossil fuel imports and achieve energy security.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following pairs regarding India's space missions:
1. PSLV: Primarily used for launching Earth Observation and remote sensing satellites.
2. GSLV: Designed for launching communication and weather satellites into geostationary transfer orbit.
3. Gaganyaan: India's first manned space mission.
How many of the above pairs are correctly matched?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three pairs are correctly matched. PSLV (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle) is ISRO's workhorse for Earth Observation satellites. GSLV (Geosynchronous Satellite Launch Vehicle) is used for heavier communication and weather satellites. Gaganyaan is indeed India's ambitious first manned space mission.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a primary objective of the PM-POSHAN (Pradhan Mantri Poshan Shakti Nirman) scheme?
options: (a) To address malnutrition among school children.
(b) To improve school enrollment and attendance.
(c) To provide vocational training to out-of-school youth.
(d) To promote community participation through 'Tithi Bhojan'.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) To provide vocational training to out-of-school youth.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The PM-POSHAN scheme (formerly Mid-Day Meal Scheme) primarily focuses on providing nutritional support to school children, improving enrollment and attendance, and fostering community involvement. Vocational training for out-of-school youth is handled by other government schemes like Skill India Mission, not PM-POSHAN.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's verdict on the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) amendments attempts to strike a balance between individual privacy and state interests. Discuss the complexities involved in this balancing act and analyze the implications of such a judgment for India's digital governance framework. (150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation; Indian Constitution—fundamental rights.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the DPDP Act and the SC judgment. Discuss the inherent tension between the right to privacy (Article 21) and legitimate state functions (national security, public order). Explain how the judgment might provide clarity but also highlight concerns about potential overreach. Conclude with implications for data fiduciaries, citizens, and future legislative actions.

mainsMocks.question: India's National Green Hydrogen Mission is a cornerstone of its energy transition strategy. Elaborate on the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India and analyze the challenges and opportunities in achieving its ambitious targets. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment; Infrastructure: Energy; Science and Technology- developments and their applications.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by defining green hydrogen and its relevance. Discuss strategic importance: energy security, climate commitments (net-zero), economic growth, global leadership. Detail opportunities: job creation, new industries, export potential, technological advancements. Address challenges: high production costs, infrastructure development, technology scaling, water availability, policy support. Conclude with a forward-looking statement on India's potential.

mainsMocks.question: Despite significant progress, gender inequality persists in India's workforce. Critically analyze the socio-economic factors contributing to this disparity and suggest comprehensive policy measures to enhance women's economic empowerment. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS1: Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues; GS2: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections.
mainsMocks.approach: Acknowledge progress but immediately highlight persistent issues like low FLFPR, wage gaps, and glass ceilings. Analyze socio-economic factors: patriarchal norms, informal care burden, lack of safe transport, skill gaps, discrimination, informalization of work. Suggest comprehensive measures: universal childcare, flexible work policies, equal pay enforcement, skill development, gender budgeting, awareness campaigns, safe public spaces, legal reforms. Conclude with the broader benefits of women's economic empowerment.

quickBytes.text: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) 2023 is India's first comprehensive law on data privacy.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) comprises six members: three from the RBI and three government nominees.
India and Japan are part of the Quad grouping, along with the United States and Australia.
Green hydrogen is produced using renewable energy, distinguishing it from grey (fossil fuels) and blue (fossil fuels with CCS) hydrogen.
PSLV is known as ISRO's 'workhorse' launch vehicle, primarily for Earth Observation satellites.
PM-POSHAN scheme is the rebranded and expanded version of the earlier Mid-Day Meal Scheme.
The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme aims to boost domestic manufacturing and exports.
The G20 is an intergovernmental forum on international economic cooperation, comprising 19 countries and the European Union.
The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) works under the Ministry of Defence, constructing roads in border areas.
NITI Aayog acts as the premier policy 'Think Tank' of the Government of India.

GS1.headline: Report Highlights Progress and Persistent Challenges in Achieving Gender Equality in Workforce
GS1.explainer:
• A new report released by a leading think tank today lauded India's progress in certain aspects of women's participation in the workforce but also pointed out persistent challenges.
• The report noted improvements in women's enrollment in higher education and entry into certain professional fields.
• However, it highlighted significant disparities in leadership roles, wage gaps, and the impact of informal care responsibilities on women's careers.
• Policy recommendations included enhanced childcare support, flexible work arrangements, and stricter enforcement of equal pay legislation.
• The findings underscore the need for continued multi-faceted interventions to achieve true gender parity in the economic sphere.
gsSubTopics: Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Social empowerment, communalism, regionalism & secularism.
prelimsPoints: Female Labour Force Participation Rate (FLFPR) measures the proportion of working-age women who are employed or actively seeking employment.
Sustainable Development Goal 5 aims to achieve gender equality and empower all women and girls.
The Equal Remuneration Act, 1976, provides for payment of equal remuneration to men and women workers for same work or work of a similar nature.
Informal care responsibilities often disproportionately fall on women, impacting their career progression.
mainsPoints: Analyze the socio-economic factors contributing to the gender gap in India's workforce and its implications for national development.
Discuss the effectiveness of existing government policies and initiatives aimed at enhancing women's economic empowerment.
Evaluate the role of education, skill development, and changing societal attitudes in promoting gender equality in the workplace.
Suggest comprehensive strategies, including legislative, infrastructural, and cultural changes, to achieve gender parity in the Indian workforce.
GS1.imageDescription: Diverse group of women professionals in a modern office setting.
GS1.trivia: According to the World Economic Forum's Global Gender Gap Report, economic participation and opportunity is one of the key pillars assessed for gender parity.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Key Provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act Amendments
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today delivered a landmark judgment, largely upholding the constitutional validity of recent amendments to the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The ruling addressed petitions challenging aspects related to data processing by state entities and the broad powers granted to the Data Protection Board.
• The court emphasized the balance between national security interests, legitimate state functions, and individual privacy rights, providing clarifications on certain clauses.
• Legal experts note the judgment's significant implications for privacy jurisprudence and the future of digital governance in India.
• This decision is expected to streamline the implementation of the DPDP Act, fostering a more robust framework for data protection.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies.
keyTerms: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act)
prelimsPoints: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) 2023 aims to regulate processing of digital personal data.
The Act establishes the Data Protection Board of India.
Key principles include purpose limitation, data minimization, and accountability.
Article 21 of the Indian Constitution guarantees the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right (Puttaswamy judgment).
mainsPoints: Discuss the balancing act between individual privacy, national security, and state functions in the digital age.
Analyze the role of the judiciary in upholding fundamental rights while allowing for legislative action on emerging technologies.
Evaluate the effectiveness and challenges of implementing data protection laws in a diverse digital economy like India's.
Examine the implications of the DPDP Act for businesses, government agencies, and individual citizens.
GS2.imageDescription: Gavel on legal documents with a blurred image of digital data streams in the background.
GS2.trivia: India's first attempt at a dedicated data protection law dates back to the Justice B.N. Srikrishna Committee report in 2018.

GS2.headline: India and Japan Deepen Strategic Partnership with New Defence and Technology Cooperation Deals
GS2.explainer:
• India and Japan today concluded a series of agreements aimed at enhancing bilateral defence cooperation, including joint development of military hardware and technology transfer.
• The deals were signed during the visit of Japan's Defence Minister to New Delhi, emphasizing shared strategic interests in the Indo-Pacific region.
• Key areas of collaboration include maritime security, cybersecurity, and emerging defence technologies.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to a free and open Indo-Pacific and discussed coordinated efforts in multilateral forums like the Quad.
• This partnership is seen as crucial for regional stability and counterbalancing growing geopolitical complexities.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and Japan are members of the Quad (Quadrilateral Security Dialogue) along with the US and Australia.
Both countries participate in joint military exercises such as 'Dharma Guardian' (army) and 'JIMEX' (navy).
Japan is a key partner in India's Act East Policy.
Indo-Pacific region is crucial for global trade and security.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India-Japan strategic partnership for regional stability in the Indo-Pacific.
Discuss how defence and technology cooperation strengthens India's 'Act East Policy' and strategic autonomy.
Evaluate the role of bilateral partnerships in multilateral frameworks like the Quad in addressing common security challenges.
Examine the potential benefits and challenges of joint defence production and technology transfer between the two nations.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian and Japanese flags superimposed over a map of the Indo-Pacific region.
GS2.trivia: Japan is a major investor in India's infrastructure projects, including the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail corridor.

GS2.headline: Uttar Pradesh MLA Sudhakar Singh Passes Away at 67
GS2.explainer:
• Sudhakar Singh, a sitting Member of Legislative Assembly (MLA) from Uttar Pradesh, passed away today at the age of 67.
• He was a prominent political figure in the region, having been elected to the state assembly in 2023.
• Singh was known for his dedication to local issues and his advocacy for rural development.
• His demise leaves a vacancy in the Uttar Pradesh Legislative Assembly, necessitating a by-election.
• Political leaders across party lines expressed condolences on his passing, acknowledging his contributions to public service.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: An MLA is a representative elected by the voters of an electoral district (constituency) to the legislature of a state government.
A by-election is held to fill a vacancy arising during the term of a current Member of Parliament or a Member of the Legislative Assembly.
The minimum age to be an MLA is 25 years.
Uttar Pradesh has a bicameral legislature, consisting of the Legislative Assembly (Vidhan Sabha) and the Legislative Council (Vidhan Parishad).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of state legislative assemblies in India's federal structure and democratic governance.
Analyze the process and implications of by-elections for political dynamics at the state level.
Examine the role and responsibilities of an MLA in representing their constituency and contributing to state policy-making.
Evaluate the challenges faced by state legislatures in effective law-making and oversight.
GS2.imageDescription: A blurred image of the Uttar Pradesh Legislative Assembly building.
GS2.trivia: The Uttar Pradesh Legislative Assembly is the largest state legislative assembly in India, with 403 elected members.

GS2.headline: Government Launches 'PM-POSHAN 2.0' for Enhanced Nutritional Support to School Children
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Government today launched 'PM-POSHAN 2.0', an upgraded and expanded version of the Mid-Day Meal Scheme, focusing on holistic nutritional support for school children.
• The scheme aims to cover all students from pre-primary to class VIII in government and government-aided schools across the country.
• Key features include an emphasis on locally sourced, nutritious food, supplementary nutrition in aspirational districts, and promoting 'Tithi Bhojan' for community participation.
• The initiative seeks to combat malnutrition, improve school enrollment and attendance, and foster better learning outcomes.
• A strong monitoring framework leveraging technology will be implemented to ensure efficient delivery and accountability.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: PM-POSHAN (Pradhan Mantri Poshan Shakti Nirman) is the new name for the Mid-Day Meal Scheme.
The scheme aims to provide hot cooked meals to children in government and government-aided schools.
It is a Centrally Sponsored Scheme.
Tithi Bhojan is a concept where people celebrate special occasions by contributing food to children in schools.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution and significance of the Mid-Day Meal Scheme in addressing child malnutrition and promoting education in India.
Analyze the potential impact of PM-POSHAN 2.0 on improving health and educational outcomes for vulnerable children.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing large-scale nutritional programs, including logistics, quality control, and community participation.
Examine the role of technology and local sourcing in enhancing the efficiency and effectiveness of such welfare schemes.
GS2.imageDescription: Children eating a meal in a school setting, depicting a welfare scheme.
GS2.trivia: The Mid-Day Meal Scheme was first launched in 1995 as a centrally sponsored scheme.

GS2.headline: India Advocates for Global South Concerns at G20 Sherpa Meeting in Brazil
GS2.explainer:
• India's G20 Sherpa today strongly advocated for the concerns and priorities of the Global South during the ongoing G20 Sherpa meeting in Brazil.
• Key issues raised included equitable access to finance, climate justice, sustainable development, and reforms of multilateral institutions.
• India emphasized the need for a human-centric approach to development and highlighted its initiatives in digital public infrastructure.
• The meeting is part of Brazil's G20 presidency preparations, focusing on building consensus for the upcoming Leaders' Summit.
• India's active participation aims to ensure that the voices of developing nations are adequately represented in global decision-making.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: G20 is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union.
The G20 Sherpa is the personal representative of the head of state or government of a G20 member country.
Brazil holds the G20 Presidency for 2025.
Global South refers to countries in Africa, Latin America, and developing Asia.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's growing role as a voice for the Global South in international forums like the G20.
Discuss the challenges faced by developing nations, such as climate financing and debt sustainability, and how G20 can address them.
Evaluate the significance of digital public infrastructure as a model for inclusive development, as advocated by India.
Examine the need for reforms in multilateral institutions to better reflect the geopolitical realities and aspirations of the Global South.
GS2.imageDescription: G20 logo with flags of member countries, representing international cooperation.
GS2.trivia: India hosted the G20 Summit in 2023, emphasizing the theme 'Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam' (One Earth, One Family, One Future).

GS2.headline: Winter Session of Parliament Expected to Prioritize Key Legislative Reforms
GS2.explainer:
• Preparations are underway for the upcoming Winter Session of Parliament, expected to commence in early December, with the government signaling a packed legislative agenda.
• Key bills anticipated for discussion and passage include reforms related to labor laws, digital infrastructure, and environmental protection.
• The opposition is gearing up to raise issues concerning economic slowdown, inflation, and federalism, promising a lively session.
• Both houses of Parliament are expected to deliberate on crucial policy matters and conduct oversight of government functioning.
• The session will be critical for advancing the government's reform agenda and addressing pressing national concerns.
gsSubTopics: Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: The Indian Parliament typically has three sessions in a year: Budget Session, Monsoon Session, and Winter Session.
The maximum gap between two sessions of Parliament cannot be more than six months.
A bill becomes an Act after being passed by both houses of Parliament and receiving presidential assent.
Question Hour and Zero Hour are important parliamentary devices for holding the government accountable.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of parliamentary sessions in India's democratic framework, particularly the Winter Session.
Analyze the role of legislative reforms in driving economic growth and social development in India.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the Parliament in ensuring effective deliberation and passage of bills amidst political differences.
Examine the mechanisms of parliamentary oversight and their importance in holding the executive accountable.
GS2.imageDescription: The Parliament House of India building at dusk.
GS2.trivia: The first Lok Sabha was constituted on 17 April 1952.

GS2.headline: India-EU Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Show Positive Momentum Amidst Key Differences
GS2.explainer:
• Negotiations for the ambitious India-European Union Free Trade Agreement (FTA) are reportedly gaining positive momentum, with both sides expressing optimism for a breakthrough.
• Senior officials from India and the EU met today to discuss key chapters, including market access for goods, services, and investment protection.
• While significant progress has been made, differences persist on issues like tariffs on certain agricultural products, intellectual property rights, and sustainability clauses.
• A successful FTA could unlock substantial economic opportunities, boosting trade and investment between two of the world's largest economies.
• Both parties reiterated their commitment to concluding a balanced and comprehensive agreement.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: A Free Trade Agreement (FTA) is a pact between two or more countries to reduce barriers to imports and exports among them.
The European Union (EU) is a political and economic union of 27 member states.
India is the EU's 10th largest trading partner.
Tariffs are taxes imposed on imported goods and services.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of a comprehensive Free Trade Agreement between India and the European Union.
Discuss the major points of contention and convergence in the ongoing India-EU FTA negotiations.
Evaluate the potential benefits and challenges for key Indian sectors if the FTA is successfully implemented.
Examine how India balances its economic interests with concerns regarding intellectual property rights and sustainability clauses in trade agreements.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and the European Union intertwined, symbolizing cooperation.
GS2.trivia: The EU is India's third-largest trading partner, accounting for 10.8% of India's total trade in goods in 2021-22.

GS2.headline: Review Meeting on National Education Policy (NEP) Implementation Highlights Progress and Gaps
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education today held a high-level review meeting to assess the progress of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation across states and institutions.
• The review highlighted significant strides in curriculum reforms, teacher training, and integrating technology into education.
• However, challenges remain in infrastructure development, digital divide in remote areas, and achieving universal foundational literacy and numeracy.
• The meeting emphasized the need for accelerated efforts, increased budgetary allocation, and greater collaboration between central and state governments.
• NEP 2020 aims to transform India's education system to meet the demands of the 21st century.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: The National Education Policy 2020 replaced the 1986 National Policy on Education.
NEP 2020 aims for universalization of education from pre-school to secondary level.
It proposes a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure.
Foundational Literacy and Numeracy (FLN) is a key focus area under NEP 2020.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of the National Education Policy 2020 in revamping India's education system.
Analyze the progress and challenges encountered in the implementation of NEP 2020 across different levels of education.
Evaluate the role of technology and teacher training in achieving the goals of foundational literacy and holistic learning.
Examine the importance of federal cooperation and adequate funding in ensuring the successful and equitable implementation of national education policies.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom engaged in learning, symbolizing education reform.
GS2.trivia: NEP 2020 aims to increase the Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) in higher education to 50% by 2035.

GS3.headline: RBI Monetary Policy Committee Maintains Repo Rate Amidst Persistent Inflation Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) today announced its decision to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• The decision was taken unanimously, citing persistent inflationary pressures, particularly from food prices, despite a moderation in core inflation.
• RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das reiterated the central bank's commitment to withdrawing accommodation gradually to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Analysts expect this stance to continue for the near term, with potential rate cuts only if inflation shows sustained decline and global economic conditions stabilize.
• The MPC also maintained its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance, signaling caution in liquidity management.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary policy.
prelimsPoints: Repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member body responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate in India.
The primary objective of monetary policy in India is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Inflation target for RBI is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth in the current global and domestic scenario.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools like the repo rate in managing demand-side inflation in India.
Evaluate the impact of persistent food inflation on household budgets and overall economic stability.
Examine the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance and its implications for liquidity in the financial system.
GS3.imageDescription: RBI logo with graphs showing economic indicators in the background.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee was constituted in 2016 to bring more transparency and accountability to monetary policy decisions.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils Ambitious New Targets for National Green Hydrogen Mission by 2030
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet today approved revised and more ambitious targets for India's National Green Hydrogen Mission, aiming for significant production capacity by 2030.
• The updated plan seeks to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, reducing reliance on fossil fuels.
• New incentives for research and development, manufacturing of electrolysers, and development of green hydrogen corridors were announced.
• The mission is expected to create numerous jobs, attract substantial investment, and contribute significantly to India's climate goals.
• Emphasizing a phased approach, the government aims to reduce the cost of green hydrogen and make it competitive with conventional fuels.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
India launched the National Green Hydrogen Mission in 2023.
The mission aims to reduce fossil fuel imports and achieve net-zero emissions by 2070.
Electrolysers are key components in green hydrogen production.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate commitments.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
Evaluate the potential impact of the National Green Hydrogen Mission on India's economy, job creation, and industrial landscape.
Examine the role of government policies and incentives in fostering a competitive green hydrogen ecosystem.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen plant with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, but green hydrogen is specifically produced using renewable energy sources.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite for Enhanced Mapping
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today successfully launched a new advanced Earth Observation Satellite (EOS) onboard the PSLV-C59 rocket from Sriharikota.
• The satellite, designated EOS-08, is equipped with high-resolution optical sensors for detailed mapping and monitoring of Earth's surface.
• Its applications include urban planning, disaster management, agricultural assessment, and environmental monitoring.
• This mission further strengthens India's remote sensing capabilities and its efforts in providing crucial geospatial data for various national developmental programs.
• The launch marks another milestone for ISRO in expanding its fleet of operational Earth observation satellites.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: PSLV is ISRO's third-generation launch vehicle, primarily used for launching Earth Observation and remote sensing satellites.
Earth Observation Satellites provide data for various applications like weather forecasting, agriculture, disaster management.
ISRO is headquartered in Bengaluru.
Remote sensing involves acquiring information about an object or phenomenon without making physical contact.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multifarious applications of Earth Observation Satellites in India's developmental planning and disaster management.
Analyze how ISRO's indigenous space capabilities contribute to India's self-reliance in critical technologies and national security.
Evaluate the role of geospatial data and remote sensing in achieving sustainable development goals in India.
Examine the challenges and future prospects for India's space program in the context of increasing private sector participation and global competition.
GS3.imageDescription: PSLV rocket launching into space with an Earth Observation Satellite.
GS3.trivia: The PSLV holds a world record for launching the highest number of satellites (104) in a single mission in 2017.

GS3.headline: India's Merchandise Exports Show Resilience Despite Global Headwinds, Data Indicates
GS3.explainer:
• Latest trade data released today indicates that India's merchandise exports have demonstrated notable resilience, registering a modest growth despite a challenging global economic environment.
• Non-petroleum and non-gem & jewellery sectors were key contributors to this positive trend, signaling diversification of India's export basket.
• Government initiatives like Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes and efforts to reduce logistics costs are attributed to supporting exporters.
• However, concerns remain regarding demand slowdown in major economies and ongoing geopolitical tensions.
• The Ministry of Commerce expects continued growth, focusing on new markets and product categories.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
Investment models.
prelimsPoints: Merchandise exports refer to the export of physical goods.
Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes aim to boost domestic manufacturing and exports in specific sectors.
Balance of Trade is the difference between a country's exports and imports of goods.
Global headwinds include factors like inflation, recession fears, and geopolitical conflicts affecting global trade.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to the resilience of India's merchandise exports amidst global economic uncertainties.
Discuss the role of government policies, such as PLI schemes and trade agreements, in boosting India's export competitiveness.
Evaluate the challenges faced by Indian exporters in diversifying markets and products, and suggest policy interventions.
Examine the impact of global trade protectionism and geopolitical shifts on India's export strategy and economic growth.
GS3.imageDescription: Shipping containers stacked at a port, symbolizing global trade.
GS3.trivia: India aims to achieve merchandise exports of $1 trillion by 2030.

GS3.headline: Central Government Unveils New Policy for Urban Wetland Conservation and Management
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change today launched a comprehensive new policy aimed at the conservation and sustainable management of urban wetlands across India.
• The policy emphasizes integrated planning, community participation, and scientific restoration of these vital ecosystems within city limits.
• It mandates the identification, mapping, and notification of urban wetlands, along with guidelines for preventing encroachment and pollution.
• Urban wetlands play a crucial role in flood mitigation, groundwater recharge, biodiversity conservation, and climate regulation.
• The initiative seeks to strengthen the ecological resilience of urban areas and enhance their 'blue-green' infrastructure.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
prelimsPoints: Wetlands are areas where water covers the soil, or is present either at or near the surface of the soil all year or for varying periods of time during the year.
The Ramsar Convention on Wetlands is an international treaty for the conservation and sustainable use of wetlands.
Urban wetlands provide critical ecosystem services like flood control, water purification, and biodiversity support.
The Wetlands (Conservation and Management) Rules, 2017, provide a regulatory framework in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological and socio-economic significance of urban wetlands, particularly in the context of increasing urbanization in India.
Analyze the challenges in conserving urban wetlands, including encroachment, pollution, and lack of public awareness.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the new policy in ensuring sustainable management and restoration of these vital ecosystems.
Examine how urban wetland conservation can contribute to climate resilience and the development of 'smart cities' in India.
GS3.imageDescription: A serene urban wetland with reeds and migratory birds, flanked by city buildings in the distance.
GS3.trivia: World Wetlands Day is celebrated annually on February 2nd, marking the date of the adoption of the Convention on Wetlands in Ramsar, Iran, in 1971.

GS3.headline: Border Roads Organisation Completes Construction of Strategic All-Weather Tunnel in Himalayas
GS3.explainer:
• The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) today announced the completion of a crucial all-weather strategic tunnel in the rugged Himalayan terrain, significantly improving connectivity to border areas.
• The tunnel, located at a high altitude, will reduce travel time, enhance troop movement, and facilitate the transport of essential supplies throughout the year.
• This infrastructure project is part of India's broader strategy to strengthen border infrastructure and improve logistical capabilities along sensitive frontiers.
• It is expected to boost economic development in remote regions by enabling easier access for trade and tourism.
• The BRO utilized advanced engineering techniques to overcome challenging geological and climatic conditions during construction.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
