Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) constructs and maintains road networks in border areas and friendly neighboring countries.
BRO works under the Ministry of Defence.
Strategic tunnels like this are vital for ensuring year-round connectivity in snow-bound and high-altitude regions.
Improved border infrastructure is crucial for national security and economic development of border regions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of developing all-weather connectivity in India's border regions, particularly in the Himalayas.
Analyze the role of the Border Roads Organisation (BRO) in enhancing India's defence preparedness and logistical capabilities.
Evaluate the socio-economic impact of improved infrastructure on the lives of people residing in remote border areas.
Examine the challenges involved in constructing infrastructure in mountainous terrain and the technological advancements employed by BRO.
GS3.imageDescription: A newly constructed tunnel entrance in a mountainous, snow-capped region.
GS3.trivia: The Atal Tunnel, also constructed by BRO, is the world's longest highway tunnel above 10,000 feet.

GS3.headline: NITI Aayog Releases Draft AI Ethics Guidelines for Public Consultation
GS3.explainer:
• NITI Aayog today released a comprehensive set of draft guidelines on Artificial Intelligence (AI) ethics for public consultation, focusing on responsible AI development and deployment.
• The guidelines emphasize principles such as fairness, accountability, transparency, safety, and privacy in AI systems.
• They aim to foster innovation while mitigating potential risks associated with AI, including bias, discrimination, and misuse.
• The framework seeks to provide a common understanding and best practices for stakeholders across government, industry, and academia.
• This move is part of India's broader strategy to become a global leader in ethical and responsible AI.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: NITI Aayog is the premier policy 'Think Tank' of the Government of India.
AI ethics refers to the branch of ethics that studies the ethical issues raised by artificial intelligence.
Principles of responsible AI often include fairness, transparency, accountability, and privacy.
Algorithmic bias occurs when an algorithm produces results that are systematically prejudiced.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ethical challenges posed by the rapid advancement and deployment of Artificial Intelligence in various sectors.
Analyze the significance of NITI Aayog's draft AI ethics guidelines in shaping India's approach to responsible AI development.
Evaluate the balance between fostering innovation in AI and mitigating potential risks such as bias and privacy violations.
Examine how a robust AI ethics framework can contribute to public trust and ensure equitable benefits from AI technologies.
GS3.imageDescription: Abstract representation of AI neural networks with a human hand reaching out, symbolizing ethical interaction.
GS3.trivia: NITI Aayog has been instrumental in formulating India's national strategy for Artificial Intelligence, emphasizing 'AI for All'.

GS3.headline: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Textiles Attracts Significant Investment Proposals
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Textiles today announced that the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Textiles has attracted a significant number of investment proposals, indicating strong industry interest.
• The scheme, aimed at boosting domestic manufacturing and exports of Man-Made Fibre (MMF) apparel, MMF fabrics, and technical textiles, is showing promising early results.
• The investments are expected to create new jobs, enhance India's competitiveness in the global textile market, and move towards a higher value chain.
• Government support through the PLI scheme is crucial for modernizing the textile sector and attracting both domestic and foreign investment.
• This initiative aligns with the 'Make in India' vision and aims to position India as a global textile manufacturing hub.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
Investment models.
prelimsPoints: The PLI scheme offers incentives on incremental sales from products manufactured in India.
It aims to make Indian manufacturing globally competitive.
Man-Made Fibres (MMF) include polyester, viscose, nylon, etc.
Technical textiles are functional fabrics used in various industries like automotive, medical, and agriculture.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme and its potential to transform India's manufacturing sector, specifically textiles.
Analyze the challenges faced by the Indian textile industry and how the PLI scheme addresses them to boost competitiveness and exports.
Evaluate the socio-economic impact of increased investment and job creation in the textile sector, particularly for women's employment.
Examine the role of government support in moving India up the global value chain in key manufacturing sectors.
GS3.imageDescription: Textile machinery in a modern factory setting.
GS3.trivia: India is one of the largest producers of textiles and apparel in the world, with the industry being a significant employer.

GS3.headline: India's Q2 GDP Growth Figures Indicate Robust Economic Momentum, Analysts Say
GS3.explainer:
• Preliminary estimates for India's Q2 (July-September) GDP growth, released today, suggest robust economic momentum, exceeding many analyst expectations.
• Key drivers included strong domestic demand, a revival in manufacturing, and healthy performance in the services sector.
• The figures provide a positive outlook amidst global economic uncertainties, reinforcing India's position as a fast-growing major economy.
• Government policy support, capital expenditure, and private consumption are cited as crucial factors contributing to this growth.
• While inflation remains a concern, the growth numbers offer confidence in India's economic resilience.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
prelimsPoints: Gross Domestic Product (GDP) is the total monetary or market value of all the finished goods and services produced within a country's borders in a specific time period.
India's financial year runs from April 1 to March 31. Q2 refers to the July-September quarter.
Domestic demand is a key component of GDP, reflecting consumption and investment within the country.
The National Statistical Office (NSO) under the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation releases GDP data.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key factors driving India's economic growth in the current fiscal year, particularly the role of domestic demand and government expenditure.
Discuss the challenges posed by global economic slowdown and inflation in sustaining India's growth momentum.
Evaluate the significance of robust GDP growth for employment generation, poverty reduction, and overall development goals in India.
Examine the policy measures that can further boost manufacturing and services sectors to ensure inclusive and sustainable economic expansion.
GS3.imageDescription: Upward trending graph representing economic growth with Indian currency notes.
GS3.trivia: India is projected to become the world's third-largest economy by 2027.
